items.title: Malaysia and Thailand end seafood dispute with landmark agriculture MoU
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia and Thailand have resolved outstanding issues related to fisheries market access and agreed to bring into force the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on Agricultural Cooperation within one week. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Anutin witnessed the exchange of the MoU between Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and his Thai counterpart Suriya Juangroongruangkit. Speaking at the joint press conference, Anwar said the agreement reflected both countries’ commitment to strengthening food security and expanding bilateral trade.

items.title: MoE to build eight new special education schools
items.summary: Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the ministry had also received approval for the construction of eight new Special Education Schools, comprising one project under the 11th Malaysia Plan (11MP), five under 12MP and two under 13MP. Wong said the ministry was also utilising the Industrialised Building System modular construction system to accelerate the delivery of special education infrastructure. Wong said 7,406 schools nationwide were implementing special education programmes as of May 31, comprising 35 special education schools, 2,907 schools offering the Special Education Integrated Programme and 4,406 schools implementing the Inclusive Education Programme.

items.title: Halal Home Grown Champion programme generates RM187.91 million potential sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Halal Home Grown Champion — Sourcing Partnership 2.0 programme has assisted 313 halal companies, generating RM187.91 million in potential sales between 2024 and this year. The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry said the beneficiaries included 158 Bumiputera-owned firms and 52 women-owned businesses. The world's largest halal trade show is scheduled to run from Sept 23 to 26 at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Johor election: How to find your right polling centre this weekend
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Registered voters for the 16th Johor state election have been advised to check their designated polling centres and voting streams ahead of polling day this Saturday. The Election Commission (EC) said voters can do so through the MySPR Semak portal or the MySPR Semak mobile application. “Let’s fulfil our responsibility as voters in the 16th Johor state election,” the EC said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: MACC to station integrity officer at Perkeso following Daya Kerjaya 2.0 fraud probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will immediately station a Certified Integrity Officer (CeIO) at the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso). The move follows governance weaknesses uncovered during investigations into the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive fraud case. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said Perkeso had requested an integrity officer to be placed at the agency after the case came to light.

items.title: THMY builds Penang factory to meet AI, data centre demand
items.summary: Purpose-built to support higher production volumes, the factory will initially house 20 advanced automated test solution workstations, enabling THMY to meet increasingly sophisticated customer testing requirements. THMY, an automated test solutions provider for the electrical and electronics (E&E) industry, said the investment will strengthen the company's ability to capitalise on the growing demand for automated test solutions, particularly across data centre, artificial intelligence and cloud computing applications, while reinforcing its position within the regional E&E manufacturing ecosystem. "The new factory will significantly enhance our production capacity and engineering capabilities.

items.title: Artificial cloud brightening could tame El Nino, but with risks
items.summary: A brewing "Super" El Nino cycle is poised to unleash heat waves, floods and drought worldwide, with the effects amplified by long term human-caused climate change. That's the premise of a new Science Advances study published Wednesday, which uses computer models to show that artificially brightening clouds over the Pacific, when timed right, could neutralise the influential weather pattern and blunt its worst impacts. They used a powerful forecasting model to gauge what impact artificial marine cloud brightening over a vast rectangular zone in the equatorial Pacific would have had on the 1997-1998 and 2015-16 El Nino events.

items.title: MACC clears ex-minister Nik Nazmi of suspicious fund transfers in Dara, Amoi, Kelat elephant case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no evidence of any suspicious fund transfers or financial transactions involving former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad over allegations that he received RM53 million in bribes linked to the transfer of three elephants – Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) – to Japan. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said preliminary checks through the Cash Threshold Report (CTR) and Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) confirmed that no suspicious financial transactions involving the former minister or companies linked to him had been detected. “Based on investigations conducted so far, there is no evidence of any fund transfers or suspicious financial transactions involving the former minister or companies associated with him.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC freezes RM3.4mil in company accounts in RM20mil false documents probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has frozen company accounts containing RM3.4 million as part of an investigation into the alleged submission of false documents to obtain about RM20 million in financing from a development financial institution. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the funds were frozen in the course of the investigation. It was previously reported that the MACC arrested three individuals, including two company directors, over the alleged submission of the documents.

items.title: Australia, India strike deal on uranium exports during Modi visit
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Australia and India reached a deal on Thursday to export Australian uranium to India for use in the nuclear energy industry, while agreeing to deepen cooperation in renewables, critical minerals and green hydrogen. Modi said on Thursday India's relationship with Australia presented "historic opportunities" for both countries to cooperate across several areas. Australia's technology, capital and resources could help accelerate India's energy transition, Modi said.

items.title: Court stays Bar's challenge to Zahid's DNAA pending Federal Court appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court adjourned the Malaysian Bar's judicial review hearing challenging the discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) granted to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi pending his Federal Court appeal in October. Judge Alice Loke granted the adjournment following a joint application by senior federal counsel Ahmad Hanir Hambaly @ Arwi and Datuk Ambiga Sreenevasan, who leads the Bar's legal team. Loke fixed Oct 12 for case management to update the court on the Federal Court proceedings.

items.title: Singapore police arrest man over bomb threats targeting Parliament, HTX
items.summary: SINGAPORE: A 40-year-old man was arrested for his suspected involvement in two anonymous bomb threats targeting Parliament House and the Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX) office, Singapore police said. The police said HTX received an online form submission on July 3 stating that a bomb had been placed in its building. Separately, police received a report that an anonymous email had been sent to the Prime Minister's Office stating that a bomb had been placed at Parliament House on July 7.

items.title: Malaysian Bar’s challenge to Zahid’s Yayasan Akalbudi DNAA postponed to October
items.summary: Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching granted the adjournment after the lead counsel for the Malaysian Bar, Datuk Ambiga Sreenevasan, informed the court that the Federal Court had fixed Oct 8 to hear the applications by Ahmad Zahid and the Attorney-General (AG) for leave to appeal against a decision made by the Court of Appeal on May 7 this year. The court then fixed Oct 12 for case management to update the outcome of the Federal Court proceedings. The Court of Appeal had overturned the High Court’s decision on June 27, 2024, which had earlier rejected the Malaysian Bar’s application for leave to initiate the judicial review.

items.title: Married couple among five nabbed in RM13.3mil ganja buds bust
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said police seized 166.2kg of ganja buds worth RM13.34 million during the raids. "Checks on both bags found 60 plastic packets containing compressed lumps believed to be ganja buds weighing about 33.6kg," he said in a statement on Wednesday. "Checks found 39 plastic packets containing compressed lumps believed to be ganja buds weighing 21.1kg, with the seized drugs estimated to be worth RM1.7 million," he said.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand to resolve fisheries market access issue within a week
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia and Thailand have agreed to resolve outstanding fisheries market access issues by expediting the implementation of a related memorandum of understanding (MoU) within a week. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said both countries' ministers had committed to fast-tracking the agreement to ensure a swift resolution. Both ministers initially proposed implementing the memorandum within one-and-a-half months.

items.title: Bank Negara keeps OPR at 2.75pct as growth, inflation stay balanced
items.summary: The central bank said the current monetary policy stance remains appropriate to support sustainable economic growth while preserving price stability. In a statement following its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, Bank Negara said Malaysia's economy remained resilient in the second quarter. Bank Negara said growth would continue to be underpinned by strong labour market conditions, wage growth and policy measures that support household spending.

items.title: Malaysia's IPI rose 8.4pct in May, says DOSM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The industrial production index (IPI) rose 8.4 per cent year-on-year (YoY) in May driven by strong growth across manufacturing, mining and electricity sectors. The Statistics Department said that the manufacturing sector rose 6.6 per cent in May, coupled with a 19.8 per cent climb in the production of the mining sector. The electricity sector grew 4.2 per cent.

items.title: Energy Commission strengthens strategic ties with Media Prima
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Energy Commission (ST) conducted a corporate visit to Media Prima Bhd today to strengthen strategic ties and boost public awareness regarding energy sector utilisation. Veliana said the collaboration with the media aimed to bring energy literacy narratives to a wider public audience. Also present were Harian Metro group editor Husain Jahit, New Straits Times deputy group editor Sharanjit Singh, BH senior news editor Datuk Sarihah Mohd Ali, and Media Prima Omnia sales general manager (government) Shanaz Md Azali.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Police rule out bullying, cite ‘unresolved grievance’ as likely motive
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 9 — Police believe that an unresolved grievance toward an acquaintance led a Form Three student to stab a classmate at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, last Monday. Selangor Police Chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar clarified that initial investigations rule out bullying as a motive, Bernama reported. Speaking at a press conference at the Selangor Contingent Police Headquarters today, Shazeli noted that other contributing factors include severe stress experienced by the 15-year-old suspect.

items.title: Johor polls: Ground-up governance drives Johor's economic growth, says Jafni
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor's economic growth was driven by a ground-up governance approach that prioritised the needs of the people and development, said caretaker state Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Md Jafni Md Shukor. He said the model had helped strengthen investor confidence and drive economic growth in the state. Jafni also credited Johor caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's 3T1M governance model for transforming Johor into Malaysia's fastest-growing state economy and attracting record investments.

items.title: Zahid tells unity allies to move on from Najib-Rosmah issues in Johor campaign
items.summary: KLUANG: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi appeared to rebuke a fellow unity government component over the use of Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor's image in Johor election campaigning. The deputy prime minister said politicians should stop revisiting issues involving Rosmah and former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, and instead focus on matters concerning Johor. "This is Johor.

items.title: PJ MP calls for comprehensive audit on outsourced IT contracts, including FWCMS
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A government backbencher has called for a comprehensive audit of all Information Technology (IT) public systems, and IT contracts outsourced to private companies, including the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS). Petaling Jaya Member of Parliament Lee Chean Chung urged the government to review these platforms after claiming that the estimated annual revenue collected through FWCMS was 21-fold compared to the Prime Minister's Department's ICT service and supply contracts. "The estimated annual revenue collected through FWCMS is about 20.87 times greater than the total value of active ICT services and supply contracts under the General Administration Division of the Prime Minister's Department.

items.title: TFP Solutions unit lands Lee Chong Wei event deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: TFP Solutions Bhd's unit Softfac Technology Sdn Bhd has secured a deal to provide exclusive payment collection and commercial infrastructure services for a live talk featuring Malaysian badminton legend Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei. TFP said Softfac had entered into a contract agreement with Ploutos, appointing it as the exclusive payment collection agent and commercial infrastructure provider for the "Beyond Greatness: From Inspiration to Impact" live talk. The agreement also covers future live concerts, festivals, shows and corporate events organised by Ploutos during the one-year agreement period.

items.title: Chow: Penang not consulted on Kedah Sultan's call to review lease payment
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang was never consulted on the Sultan of Kedah's call for the federal government to review the state's lease payment, says Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow said the matter falls under federal jurisdiction as the payment is made by Putrajaya and does not involve the state government. "There has never been any discussion because the payment is made or contributed by the federal government.

items.title: Labour leadership contest takes Burnham closer to UK PM's office
items.summary: LONDON: Veteran politician Andy Burnham will take another step towards becoming the UK's next prime minister when nominations to replace Keir Starmer as Labour leader formally open on Thursday. Burnham will need to show that he has the support of 81 of Labour's 402 MPs to become a formal candidate, a tally that he is expected to surpass easily. Burnham would then replace Starmer in 10 Downing Street on Monday 20 July.

items.title: Affin refocuses SME BizChat on execution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Affin Bank Bhd is shifting its annual Affin SME BizChat from thought leadership to practical execution. This year's programme focuses on helping small and medium enterprises (SMEs) strengthen their financial resilience amid a challenging business environment. "SMEs are central to Malaysia's economic resilience and future growth.

items.title: 70-year-old woman loses RM183,100 in love scam after befriending a 'doctor' online
items.summary: MUAR: A 70-year-old retired woman lost RM183,100 after falling victim to a love scam involving two impersonators she befriended through Facebook Messenger. The victim alleged she befriended the suspects online. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Raiz Mukhliz Azman Aziz said the victim began communicating with the two suspects in late April before they gained her trust and sought financial assistance.

items.title: Aeon Credit Q1 profit jumps 23pct to RM95.16mil on loan growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Credit Service (M) Bhd recorded a higher net profit of RM95.16 million for the first financial quarter ended May 31, 2026 (Q1 FY27), up 22.7 per cent from RM77.55 million a year earlier, supported by stronger loan and financing growth. The group said total transaction and financing volume rose 4.6 per cent year-on-year to RM2.35 billion during the quarter. Gross financing receivables stood at RM16.07 billion as at May 31, 2026, increasing RM1.44 billion, or 9.8 per cent, from a year earlier.

items.title: Four, including two brothers, plead not guilty to running online gambling centre
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Four people, including two brothers, were charged in the magistrate's court here today with managing an online gambling operation in April. Eow Yao Ping, 25, Eow Yong Huang, 30, Tan Yan Zhong, 25, and Cheng Chean Seng, 61, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before magistrate Noor Farah Hazwani Osman. They were accused of acting with common intention to manage a common gaming house used as an online gambling operations centre between 4.20pm and 6pm at Taman Kampung Raja here on April 7.

items.title: Hedge funds record best first-half performance in 13 years
items.summary: LONDON: Global hedge funds delivered their strongest first-half performance since 2013, data from hedge fund data firm PivotalPath showed, as healthcare, tech and energy trades lifted returns in volatile markets. In particular, April - when hedge funds returned 3.7 per cent in one month - proved to be the strongest April on record, the PivotalPath data showed. Stockpickers returned 4.0 per cent last month, said the Goldman note from Wednesday.

items.title: Proton expands EV push with e.Mas 5 launch in Singapore
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd has expanded its electric vehicle (EV) presence in Singapore with the launch of the Proton e.Mas 5, marking the second model under its EV brand to enter the island republic after the Proton e.Mas 7. Proton International Corp chief executive officer Edmund Lim Meng Thong said the introduction of the Proton e.Mas 5 reflects growing confidence in Malaysian-made automotive products and reinforces the company's export ambitions. "Proton's ambition goes beyond exporting vehicles.

items.title: China, Taiwan brace for Typhoon Bavi, possibly the most powerful storm in years
items.summary: TOKYO: China and Taiwan were bracing for possibly the most destructive tropical storm in years as Typhoon Bavi churned southeast of Taiwan on Thursday, with winds near 200 kph, and as parts of China were still reeling from Typhoon Maysak. Bavi, currently about 1,000 km at its widest point or roughly the width of France, is forecast to skirt northern Taiwan before making landfall in China's eastern Fujian province on Saturday evening, according to China's National Meteorological Centre. China is still reeling from Typhoon Maysak, which wreaked havoc across the southwestern region of Guangxi.

items.title: Johor not Singapore's 'backyard', says PH's Tiram candidate
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor should not be considered Singapore's "backyard", says Pakatan Harapan's Tiram candidate Nor Zulaila Abdul Ghani. She said Johor's strategic position, industrial strength and economic potential should not be overshadowed by its proximity to Singapore. Johor is Malaysia's future," she said.

items.title: Johor polls: Johor housing model delivers more than 21,000 affordable homes, says Jafni
items.summary: Jassmine Shadiqe JOHOR BARU: Affordable housing has emerged as the centrepiece of BN Bukit Permai state seat incumbent's re-election campaign. Caretaker state Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Md Jafni Md Shukor is highlighting Johor's delivery of more than 21,000 affordable homes and expanded assistance for first-time buyers as key achievements. Jafni said voters had raised housing, cost of living and local development as key issues.

items.title: Johor polls: Teo Nie Ching urges voters to return home, beware of fake news
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching has urged Johor voters, particularly those living outside the state or overseas, to exercise their democratic right by returning home to vote in the Johor state election this Saturday. I hope voters working in Kuala Lumpur or Singapore will also cherish that right and come out to vote on July 11,” she said. Meanwhile, Kartiyaini said the PH election machinery is stepping up its campaign to encourage thousands of Johor voters working in Singapore to return home to cast their ballots this Saturday.

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar urges voters to give Pakatan a chance to govern state
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has appealed to the people of Johor to give Pakatan Harapan (PH) a chance to serve the state as they prepare to cast their ballots in the 16th Johor state election this Saturday. The PH chairman said the coalition was committed to working hard to safeguard Johor’s interests if given the mandate by the people. “ Laksa Johor tiada tandingan, Warisan nenda dikenang selalu, Ayuhlah pulang membawa harapan, Makmurlah negeri bertambah maju .” The 16th Johor state election sees 172 candidates vying for 56 state seats.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC probes election candidate over alleged corruption in Johor polls
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is looking into allegations of corruption against an election candidate during the Johor election campaign. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman declined to disclose further details, saying the commission wanted to remain impartial and avoid jeopardising the ongoing election process. "If there is corruption during the election, regardless of the political party, candidate, voter or supporter involved, corruption is corruption.

items.title: Norway defence freeze justified after missile licence cancellation, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 9 — The government’s decision to halt all future procurement and contracts with Norway is justified as the country has failed to show respect for Malaysia, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said the Cabinet had endorsed the Ministry of Defence’s recommendation to stop procuring defence assets from Norway after the country revoked the export licence for a missile system ordered by Malaysia. “Once existing contracts involving Norway expire, the government may no longer engage Norwegian companies or renew those contracts.

items.title: Johor polls: Police approve over 4,000 campaign permits, probe 22 election offence cases
items.summary: Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said between July 7 and 8 alone, police received 884 permit applications, of which 838 were approved after meeting the stipulated requirements. Ab Rahaman said between July 7 and 8, police also received 17 reports and opened four investigation papers into election offences. Cumulatively, Ab Rahaman said police received 73 reports and opened 22 investigation papers related to election offences between June 27 and July 8.

items.title: Jawi: Federal Territory Muslim burials will not be privatised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Federal Territory Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) has sought to reassure the public that Muslim burial services will not be privatised under the Federal Territories Public Cemetery Development Project. Jawi director Hanifuddin Roslan said the land at Lot PT3458, in Hulu Semenyih, will remain under the Federal Lands Commissioner and has been gazetted as a 90.12-hectare (222.68-acre) public cemetery. “Once the cemetery becomes operational, Jawi will assume full responsibility for managing all burial affairs, including unclaimed bodies, burials and cemetery maintenance, as it does at the eight existing Raudhatul Sakinah Muslim cemeteries,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Perlis MB quits Bersatu supreme council to focus on governing state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah today confirmed he has resigned as a member of Bersatu’s Supreme Leadership Council (MPT) to focus fully on administering the state government. He said Sena assemblyman and state executive councillor Marzita Mansor had also submitted her resignation from the party’s top leadership body last week, according to a Berita Harian report. Abu Bakar said he would remain a Bersatu member, the party’s Perlis chairman and Kangar division chief with the consent of party president Muhyiddin Yassin.

items.title: Anwar, Thai PM Anutin hold talks to deepen Malaysia-Thailand ties
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today held a bilateral meeting with Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul at the Perdana Putra Complex here to review the progress of Malaysia-Thailand bilateral relations and discuss ways to further strengthen cooperation in various fields. Among the Malaysian Cabinet ministers present were Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan and Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. The Thai delegation comprised Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Sihasak Phuangketkeow; Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Transport Phiphat Ratchakitprakarn; Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Finance Ekniti Nitithanprapas; Minister of Tourism and Sports Surasak Phancharoenworakul; Minister of Defence Lt. General Adul Boonthumjaroen; Minister of Agriculture and Cooperatives Suriya Juangroongruangkit; and Minister of Labour Julapun Amornvivat.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng says Malaysian banks’ RM54.8b profit can fund MSME loan relief measures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The banking industry’s higher profits in 2025 show that banks can afford to provide more support for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), said Lim Guan Eng, who renewed his call for interest-free loans and a temporary loan repayment relief measure for businesses. The Bagan MP who is also DAP’s national adviser said the banking sector’s profit before tax increased by RM6.5 billion, or 13.5 per cent, to RM54.8 billion in 2025 from RM48.3 billion the previous year, arguing that the industry should play a bigger role in helping MSMEs facing rising costs and economic pressures. Lim, however, said increasing loan availability alone would not address the challenges faced by MSMEs, which he said were affected by the global economic slowdown and higher oil prices linked to the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: PAC: Delays in fleet replacement cost government nearly RM29m
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The government lost nearly RM28.79 million due to delays in replacing thousands of cars in its rented vehicle fleet, the Public Accounts Committee said today. PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said this was established after the committee investigated the government’s vehicle rental contract, which had been flagged for weaknesses in the Auditor-General’s Report last October. “The government was forced to pay the full monthly fixed rental costs alongside much higher monthly repair and maintenance rates, compared to if the vehicles had been replaced with new ones that carry significantly lower first-year maintenance costs.” PAC launched an inquiry as a follow-up to the the Auditor-General’s audit that deemed the management of concession vehicles by the government unsatisfactory, specifically panning Spanco Sdn Bhd’s delivery delays and the government’s subsequent failure to impose late penalties.

items.title: Penang draws more than half a million foreign patients, accounts for 45pc of Malaysia’s medical tourism revenue
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 9 — Penang now contributes 45 per cent of Malaysia’s total medical tourism revenue, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. He said this reaffirmed the state’s position as the country’s leading medical tourism destination. He said the state government will continue to work closely with the private healthcare sector to strengthen the state’s medical ecosystem.

items.title: Lease row: Penang CM leaves Kedah’s payment demand to federal government
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 9 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow has deferred the dispute over lease payments to the Kedah state government to Putrajaya, maintaining that the matter falls entirely under federal jurisdiction. While the federal government has paid an annual honorarium of RM10 million to Kedah on behalf of Penang since 2018, the Kedah state government is demanding a steep increase to RM100 million annually. “This is an issue for the federal government.

items.title: Alleged Gang 24 Hung Hung top member pleads not guilty in Sarawak to having over 120 rounds of ammo, firearm
items.summary: KUCHING, July 9 — A top member of a group allegedly linked to the Gang 24 Hung Hung has claimed trial here to six charges of possessing 120 rounds of ammunition without a licence and one charge of possessing a firearm. In proceedings before Sessions Court Judge Musyida Abd Halim, the 40-year-old accused faced one charge under Section 8 of the Firearms (Increased Penalties) Act 1971 for possessing a firearm without lawful authority. The accused was also charged separately before Magistrates Nursyaheeqa Nazwa Radzali and Ling Hui Chuan with six charges, each framed under Section 8(a) of the Arms Act 1960 for possessing ammunition without a valid licence or permit.

items.title: AirBorneo to offer fixed RM375 Kuching-KL fares year-round, says Abang Johari
items.summary: KUCHING, July 9 — AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) will implement a consistent, year-round rate of RM375 all-in fare for its flights on the Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route to stabilise airfares and challenge the pricing models of other commercial airlines, said Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. The Premier said the airline’s static pricing model serves as a direct intervention strategy against the steep airfare hikes commonly seen during peak travel periods and major festive seasons. “That will become a check to the other airlines because for us in Sarawak, we rely on an air bridge and air connectivity.

items.title: Nancy Shukri: Ministry reviewing Destitute Persons Act, may introduce new law for homelessness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development is reviewing the Destitute Persons Act 1977 to ensure the law remains relevant in addressing homelessness and vulnerable groups. She said this would take into account issues such as the rising number of beggars and homeless individuals, as well as the lack of dedicated facilities for homeless people with mental health conditions. Citing Social Welfare Department records, Nancy said 206 elderly individuals were taken into care nationwide under Section 3(1) of Act 183 between January and April 2026.

items.title: After outlet park in Sepang, Mitsui Fudosan and MAHB partner for RM80m aviation logistics hub in Subang
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA, July 9 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook has hailed a renewed partnership between Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB) and Mitsui Fudosan, signalling a strategic push to cement Malaysia’s position as a regional aerospace hub. The collaboration centres on a new RM80 million joint venture to develop a maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) logistics complex in Subang. “A lot of Malaysians know Mitsui because of Mitsui Outlet Park.

items.title: Jho Low back in Malaysia? Putrajaya tells Parliament: Nothing to it
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — Putrajaya today denied rumours that fugitive Malaysian businessman Jho Low had returned to the country recently with a delegation from China to negotiate a settlement of 1Malaysia Development Berhad’s (1MDB) multi-billion ringgit debt. The rumour was first carried by news portal Sarawak Report, which claimed that the wanted Penang-born – whose real name is Low Taek Jho – had entered the country to discuss a purported “repayment package”. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong refuted the report in Parliament this morning when it was raised by Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman.

items.title: Thailand PM Anutin arrives in Malaysia for two-day official visit
items.summary: SEPANG, July 9 — Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul arrived in Malaysia today for a two-day official visit at the invitation of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Also present to welcome Anutin and his spouse were the Prime Minister’s wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, who was the receiving minister for the visit. This is Anutin’s first visit to Malaysia since his reappointment as Prime Minister in March 2026.

items.title: Young Malaysians delaying marriage amid cost pressures, career uncertainty, says LPPKN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — Economic pressures, job mismatches, and challenges in building a career have been identified as factors driving some young people to delay marriage, according to the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN). Director of the Research, Population and Family Division Adzmel Mahmud said young people therefore need continued support from the government, employers, educational institutions and families to help them realise their aspirations of building a family. Adzmel said mental, physical and social wellbeing also play an important role in helping young people make decisions about their careers, marriage and family formation.

items.title: Johor polls: Free buses, extra ETS seats offered for outstation voters
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — A non-governmental organisation, Stesen Pemantauan Rakyat, is providing six free buses to transport 240 voters from Kuala Lumpur and Singapore home to cast their ballots in the Johor state election this Saturday. “Buses from Kuala Lumpur to Johor will depart at 9pm on Friday. KTMB Group chief executive officer Datuk Azlan Shah Al Bakri said an additional 7,560 seats have been added for the KL Sentral-JB Sentral-KL Sentral route, bringing total capacity to 15,120 seats.

items.title: Liew Chin Tong: Malaysia still on the hook for RM20b in 1MDB debt, with RM51b total liabilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The government remains liable for over RM20 billion of 1MDB’s debt if it fails to recover further assets, despite having already paid up to RM40 billion in one of the world's largest embezzlement scandals. “In addition, the government remains responsible for covering the Government-guaranteed Islamic Medium-Term Note (IMTN) Sukuk amounting to RM8.9 billion,” Liew said during Question Time. “This sukuk comprises RM5 billion in principal payments and RM3.9 billion in interest, which will mature in 2039.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues advisory on Typhoon Bavi detected near Sabah, says no threat to Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an advisory on Typhoon Bavi, which was detected about 1,896 kilometres (km) north-east of Sandakan, Sabah. In a statement today, MetMalaysia said it was located approximately 1,151 km south-east of Taipei, Taiwan. The department said that based on observation at 8 am, the typhoon was moving west-north-west at a speed of 20 km per hour (km/h), with maximum wind speeds potentially reaching 185 km/h.

items.title: Over 96,000 Form Six students to receive RM14.4m early schooling aid, says Education Ministry
items.summary: It said 52,810 new Form Six students at 616 Form Six Centres (PTEs) nationwide would each receive RM150 under the aid this year. “The distribution of the Early Schooling Aid to new Form Six students is being carried out by the Form Six Centres from June 28 to July 27. “The MOE is continuing the distribution of the Early Schooling Aid to new Form Six students who enrolled on June 8, following the approval to extend the aid to Form Six students from last year,” it said in a statement today.

items.title: Home minister says 78 Rohingya granted refugee status, 25 eligible for work under DPP pilot scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — A total of 78 Myanmar nationals of Rohingya ethnicity have been granted refugee status under Malaysia’s new Refugee Registration Document (DPP) programme, with 25 of them identified as eligible for employment under a pilot work initiative. “Until June 2026, 128 Myanmar nationals of Rohingya ethnicity, comprising 127 adult men and one adult woman, were transferred to PPKPPS (Special Detention Centre for Refugees and Asylum Seekers) Bidor, Perak. “Following the status determination process, 78 individuals comprising 77 men and one woman have been approved as refugees under the DPP programme.

items.title: Lindung 24 Jam contributions now optional for local employees, mandatory for foreign workers, says HR minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The government has decided that contributions to the Non-Employment Injury Scheme (Lindung 24 Jam) under the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) will no longer be mandatory for local workers and will instead be voluntary, with immediate effect. Human Resource Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan said, however, that contributions to the Lindung 24 Jam Scheme would remain mandatory for foreign workers and would continue to be implemented in accordance with existing legal provisions. “Local workers may now choose whether to contribute to the Lindung 24 Jam Scheme based on their individual needs and circumstances.

items.title: Sabah police investigate claims two primary pupils were bullied, kicked by schoolmate
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 9 — Police are investigating an alleged bullying and physical assault case involving two primary school pupils here following a report lodged by their father. Acting Kota Kinabalu Police Chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Abdul Rahman said the report was lodged on July 7, with the complainant alleging that his two children had been repeatedly bullied and physically assaulted by a male schoolmate. Syed Lot said police are carrying out further investigations, including establishing the suspect’s full identity and recording statements from the victims, witnesses, teachers and the school administration.
